members.summary: Configuration for Mask2Former (Cheng et al., CVPR 2022).
members.extended:
The field set mirrors the reference framework's `Mask2FormerConfig`
so the pretrained-weight converter is a near-identity key map.  The
pipeline is:

Image → Swin backbone → [stage1..4] feature maps
    → MSDeformAttn pixel decoder → 3 multi-scale memory levels
                                 + 1/4-scale mask features
    → 9-layer masked-attention transformer decoder (cycling levels)
    → class head (Linear → K+1) + mask head (MLP → dot mask features)

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of semantic classes (foreground; the
                   class head emits `num_classes + 1`).

parameters.name: num_decoder_layers
parameters.description:
Transformer decoder depth (the decoder uses
                   `num_decoder_layers - 1` masked layers; the
                   extra slot is the pre-layer mask prediction).

parameters.name: num_queries
parameters.description: Number of learnable object queries N.

members.name: Mask2FormerForSemanticSegmentation

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, targets: dict[str, Tensor] | None = None)

members.summary: Mask2Former universal segmentation model (Cheng et al., CVPR 2022).
members.extended:
A unified mask-classification architecture that achieves state-of-the-art
results across semantic, instance, and panoptic segmentation with the same
code path.  Its key advance over MaskFormer is **masked attention**: each
transformer decoder layer restricts cross-attention to the foreground
region of the *previous* layer's predicted mask, which sharply accelerates
convergence and improves small-object segmentation.

The pipeline is: a Swin backbone -> a multi-scale deformable-attention
pixel decoder producing per-pixel mask features at stride 4 plus three
coarser memory levels -> $L$ masked-attention decoder layers cycling
through the three memory levels with learnable query embeddings ->
sibling class and mask-embedding heads.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the Swin-backbone factories
(`mask2former_swin_tiny` / `small` / `base` / `large`)
for the paper-cited ADE20K variants.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask2former import mask2former_swin_tiny
>>> model = mask2former_swin_tiny()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 384, 384)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape   # (B, K, H, W)
(1, 150, 384, 384)
notes:
See Cheng et al., "Masked-attention Mask Transformer for Universal Image
Segmentation", CVPR 2022 ([arXiv:2112.01527](https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.01527)).  For semantic inference the
model drops the no-object slot and collapses queries via the
`softmax(class)[..., :-1] ⊗ sigmoid(mask)` einsum into a
$(B, K, H, W)$ logit map, upsampled to the input resolution.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

members.name: mask2former_swin_tiny
members.signature: mask2former_swin_tiny(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: Mask2FormerSwinTinyWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Mask2Former with Swin-Tiny backbone (Cheng et al., CVPR 2022).
members.extended:
Builds a `Mask2FormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the Swin-Tiny
backbone (`embed_dim = 96`, `depths = (2, 2, 6, 2)`,
`num_heads = (3, 6, 12, 24)`, `window_size = 7`), a 6-layer
multi-scale deformable pixel decoder, and a 9-layer masked-attention
transformer decoder with 100 queries.  Default targets ADE20K
(150 classes); reaches ADE20K validation mIoU of 47.7%.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag (`Mask2FormerSwinTinyWeights.ADE20K`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the Mask2Former-Swin-T configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask2former import mask2former_swin_tiny
>>> model = mask2former_swin_tiny()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 384, 384)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[1]
150

members.summary: Mask2Former with Swin-Small backbone (Cheng et al., CVPR 2022).
members.extended:
Builds a `Mask2FormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the Swin-Small
backbone (`embed_dim = 96`, `depths = (2, 2, 18, 2)`,
`num_heads = (3, 6, 12, 24)`).  Same transformer head as
`mask2former_swin_tiny`; ADE20K validation mIoU of 51.3%.

→ the `DEFAULT` tag; a tag string → that specific checkpoint.

(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

150

Builds a `Mask2FormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the Swin-Base
backbone (`embed_dim = 128`, `depths = (2, 2, 18, 2)`,
`num_heads = (4, 8, 16, 32)`).  ADE20K validation mIoU of 53.9%.

members.summary: Mask2Former with Swin-Large backbone (Cheng et al., CVPR 2022).
members.extended:
Builds a `Mask2FormerForSemanticSegmentation` with the largest
Swin backbone (`embed_dim = 192`, `depths = (2, 2, 18, 2)`,
`num_heads = (6, 12, 24, 48)`).  Reaches ADE20K validation mIoU of
56.1% — the headline result of the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag; a tag string → that specific checkpoint.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

returns.description:
Segmentation model with the Mask2Former-Swin-L configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask2former import mask2former_swin_large
>>> model = mask2former_swin_large()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 384, 384)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[1]
150

members.name: Mask2FormerSwinTinyWeights
Mask2FormerSwinSmallWeights
Mask2FormerSwinBaseWeights
Mask2FormerSwinLargeWeights
members.extended:
Single ADE20k semantic checkpoint converted from
`facebook/mask2former-swin-large-ade-semantic` (Cheng et al., 2022;
~215M params, 56.1 mIoU on the ADE20k validation set — the headline
paper result).

extended: Paper: "Masked-attention Mask Transformer for Universal Image Segmentation"
citation: Cheng, Bowen, et al. "Masked-attention Mask Transformer for Universal Image Segmentation." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, 2022, pp. 1290–1299.
theory:
Mask2Former generalises MaskFormer into a single architecture
that wins on **semantic**, **instance**, and **panoptic**
segmentation simultaneously.  It keeps the mask-classification
framing — $N$ object queries each predict a class and a
binary mask — and improves it with three orthogonal changes.

**Masked cross-attention.**  In every decoder layer the queries
attend only to feature locations that the *previous* layer
already considered foreground for that query:



$$
\mathrm{attn}_{ij} = \mathrm{softmax}_j\!\bigl(
    Q_i K_j^{\top} / \sqrt{d} + \mathcal{M}_{ij}^{\,\ell-1}
\bigr),
$$

where $\mathcal{M}^{\ell-1}_{ij} = 0$ if the predicted
binary mask of query $i$ at layer $\ell - 1$
activates pixel $j$ and $-\infty$ otherwise.  This
confines query updates to plausible mask regions and accelerates
convergence.

**Multi-scale features.**  The decoder layers cycle through three
pyramid levels $(P_3, P_4, P_5)$ of an enhanced FPN-style
*pixel decoder*, so high-resolution semantic detail and broad
contextual features are both available to every query.

**Improved pixel decoder.**  A multi-scale deformable-attention
encoder fuses the backbone feature maps before producing the
per-pixel embeddings against which the query embeddings are dotted
to form binary masks.

Training uses the same Hungarian-matched class CE + mask BCE/Dice
losses as MaskFormer (with auxiliary losses at intermediate
decoder layers).  Mask2Former sets a new SOTA on each of ADE20K,
Cityscapes, and COCO with a *single* model, eliminating the need
for task-specific architectures.
